assertion_results.text: The response describes locating the digest comment using the marker string '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' from shared/description-digest-protocol.md
assertion_results.evidence: Section 2 'Locate the Digest Comment Using the Marker String' describes searching for comments matching 'the marker string defined in shared/description-digest-protocol.md: [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' and notes the multiple-comments tiebreaker rule.

assertion_results.text: The response describes detecting a mismatch between the stored digest and the computed digest of the current description
assertion_results.evidence: Section 7 'Compare Hex Digests — MISMATCH DETECTED' states: 'The hex digests do not match' and displays both the expected (stored) and actual (computed) digest values.

assertion_results.text: The response alerts the user that the description was modified after plan-feature created the task and displays the expected vs actual digest values
assertion_results.evidence: Section 8 'Alert the User' displays a formatted warning: 'The description of TC-9201 has been modified since plan-feature created this task' with expected digest (sha256-md:0000...) and actual digest (sha256-md:<computed>) displayed.

assertion_results.text: The response offers the user a choice: (1) proceed with the current description, or (2) stop so they can re-run plan-feature
assertion_results.evidence: The alert presents: '1. Proceed — Continue implementing with the current (modified) description as-is' and '2. Stop — Abort implementation so you can review the changes and re-run plan-feature to regenerate tasks' with a 'Choose (1/2):' prompt.

assertion_results.text: The response stops execution and does not proceed to Step 2 or implementation planning until the user responds — following the same pause-and-ask pattern as the incomplete description handling
assertion_results.evidence: Section 9 'STOP Execution — Await User Response' explicitly states: 'Execution halts immediately at this point' and lists all steps (2-11) that are NOT executed. References 'the same pause-and-ask pattern used for incomplete description handling in Step 1'.
